label: cold_P5.9
answer:
Your last cholesterol reading from April 5, 2026 shows:

Total Cholesterol: 237 mg/dL (higher than ideal)
LDL: 162 mg/dL (the "bad" cholesterol — elevated)
HDL: 38 mg/dL (the "good" cholesterol — low)
Triglycerides: 186 mg/dL (elevated)

These numbers suggest increased cardiovascular risk, which is why you were referred to a cardiologist.

Regarding your HbA1c:
Your most recent HbA1c from April 5, 2026 is 6.1%, which puts you in the prediabetic range. This hasn't improved from your earlier February reading (also 6.1%), so it's stayed stable rather than improved.
This means your average blood sugar over the past few months is higher than normal, but you don't yet have diabetes. It's a signal to focus on lifestyle — diet, exercise, and weight management can make a real difference.

Not medical advice — talk to your doctor before acting on this.
